title: Tapping to Calm Body Stress and Accept Now
subtitle: An EFT sequence for calming the stressed body and accepting where you are right now
excerpt: An EFT tapping practice for the body that is running on stress: the tight chest, the braced shoulders, the low-level alarm that has become the background setting. Works through the stress patterns toward accepting the body as it is today.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s). Draws on the body-stress work in Rebecca J Page's 84-day programme.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the body is tense and braced, particularly first thing in the morning or after a stressful interaction.

content.text: A five-minute EFT tapping practice for the body that has been holding stress. The script addresses the physical bracing pattern and the habit of pushing from an uncomfortable baseline, moving toward acceptance of exactly where the body is right now.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point and say each statement once:
Even though my body is carrying a lot of stress and I don't know how to put it down, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I keep bracing for the next thing and my body is tense and ready to react, I accept where I am right now.
Even though I am not where I want to be with my body, I am willing to begin from here.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: The stress that has settled into the body.
Side of Eye: The shoulders already up before the day begins.
Under Eye: My body is braced for what comes next.
Under Nose: The body as an alarm that never fully switches off.
Chin: I am not where I want to be and that feels heavy.
Collarbone: The gap between here and where I think I should be.
Under Arm: Carrying the weight of the starting point.
Top of Head: The body that is doing its best from where it is.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: My body is allowed to be exactly as it is right now.
Side of Eye: I can begin from here. Here is the only place I can begin.
Under Eye: The stress can start to ease. I am not in danger right now.
Under Nose: My body is working for me, even when it feels against me.
Chin: I accept my body as my starting point today.
Collarbone: I let the shoulders drop. I let the chest soften.
Under Arm: Calm is something my body knows how to do.
Top of Head: I am here. That is enough to start with.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Written for homemade.education. The EFT framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, in use since the mid-1990s.
